text.title: gce-instance-templates Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gce-instance-templates. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Instance templates MUST use an image family (e.g. ubuntu-os-cloud/ubuntu-2404-lts-amd64) rather than a specific image version. Templates MUST be versioned by name suffix (date or semantic version, e.g. app-template-20260427 or app-template-v2). The lifecycle block MUST contain create_before_destroy = true in Terraform.
rule.rationale: Image families resolve to the latest patched image at creation time, eliminating the need to update the template for OS security patches. Name versioning enables audit trails and rollback. create_before_destroy prevents Terraform from deleting the in-use template before the replacement is ready, which would break MIG updates.

rule.statement: Instance templates MUST attach a dedicated service account with only the IAM roles required for the workload (principle of least privilege). Templates MUST NOT use the default Compute service account. The scopes field MUST be set to cloud-platform when a dedicated service account is used (IAM roles on the SA govern access, not scopes).
rule.rationale: The default Compute service account has project editor permissions; any compromised VM becomes a full project compromised. A dedicated SA with minimal roles limits blast radius. The cloud-platform scope defers to SA IAM roles and avoids the confusion of per-API scope lists.
